name: Rovables
short_description: Miniature untethered robots that drive freely over a person's clothing using magnetic wheels, turning wearables into relocatable, mobile on-body devices.
long_description: Rovables are centimeter-scale robots that move across unmodified clothing, gripping the fabric between magnetic wheels so they can even climb vertically. Each robot is untethered, with an onboard battery, microcontroller, wireless radio, and a low-power localization system based on wheel encoders and an IMU that supports limited autonomous navigation on the body. By making wearables mobile rather than fixed, the project opens an interaction design space for on-body sensing, modular displays, tactile feedback, and interactive clothing and jewelry, reframing the body itself as a navigable surface for physical devices.
ai_note: Navigation relies on wheel-encoder and IMU odometry plus magnetic-wheel actuation; the paper describes no machine learning or AI component.

images.caption: A Rovables prototype with magnetic wheels climbing a piece of vertical clothing.
images.alt_text: A small two-wheeled robot the size of a coin grips and climbs a vertical piece of fabric, held against the cloth by its wheels.

sources.title: Rovables: Miniature On-Body Robots as Mobile Wearables
Rovables: Miniature On-Body Robots as Mobile Wearables (MIT Media Lab publication page)
Rovables: Miniature On-Body Robots as Mobile Wearables (open-access PDF)

interactions.title: Robots climb to your joints and reconstruct your arm on screen
interactions.description: Several Rovables drive themselves to positions on the arm and use their onboard IMUs as a motion-capture rig; the authors fed the readings through inverse-kinematics equations to redraw the limb's pose live on a monitor, sidestepping the usual 17-sensor manual placement.
interactions.source_label: Rovables UIST 2016 paper (PDF) — Body motion tracking

interactions.title: A wrist watch that walks to your chest to become a name tag
interactions.description: A Rovable carrying an RGB-LED display shield shows an analog watch face on the wrist, then drives to the chest and magnetically links with a second display robot to form a larger name-tag screen, and re-forms as bicycle turn lights on the back.

interactions.title: A poking tactor that can buzz you anywhere on the body
interactions.description: With a rack-and-pinion linear actuator mounted on top, a Rovable physically pokes the skin with about 1N of force, and because it can relocate it delivers that tactile cue at whatever spot on the body is needed rather than a fixed wristband.

interactions.title: A brooch that slides up to your collar to take a call
interactions.description: Wearing an aesthetic cover, a Rovable sits as a decorative brooch and then, when a phone call comes in, drives up toward the neck to act as a shoulder microphone/speaker for hands-free talking.
interactions.source_label: Rovables UIST 2016 paper (PDF) — Interactive moving jewelry

interactions.title: Clothing that reshapes itself when it gets warm
interactions.description: Fitted with a small Velcro hook, a Rovable grips the ends of a shawl and pulls them so the garment shape-changes into a scarf in response to a temperature change, turning the robot into an actuator of the clothing itself.

interactions.title: Park on a hidden coil to recharge and re-zero its position
interactions.description: A 0.5mm inductive coil on the undercarriage lets a Rovable drive to a Qi charging pad taped behind the fabric (a phone in a pocket can serve as the base), top up its battery, and reset the accumulated dead-reckoning drift using the IR beacon at that home spot.
interactions.source_label: Rovables UIST 2016 paper (PDF) — Wireless charging & localization

interactions.title: Migrate a heart-rate sensor to its own best spot
interactions.description: Because a Rovable carries a sensor and knows its position via wheel-encoder and IMU odometry, it could crawl across the chest sampling as it goes and settle where the signal is cleanest, rather than relying on a person to place a fixed sensor correctly.

interactions.title: A swarm that disperses into health sensors and reassembles as a wristwatch
interactions.description: A handful of fingernail-sized Rovables could scatter across a shirt to log motion and physiology through the day, then converge onto the wrist to render a watch face the moment you glance down, using the same magnetic locomotion and modular-display linking the prototype already demonstrates.
